# AI Agent Payment Safety Stack

This project provides AI agent control primitives for paid API calls, budget checks,
evidence review, gate routing, human review, and approval unit generation.

## Base URL

https://ai-agent-payment-safety-stack.onrender.com

## Available API

### POST https://ai-agent-payment-safety-stack.onrender.com/api/approval-unit/build

Builds a minimal human decision contract from an AI-generated candidate.

**Core concept: Approval Unit = Human Decision Contract**

Use this endpoint when an AI agent needs to convert:
- security findings
- patch candidates
- remediation plans
- x402 / JPYC / USDC payment requests
- deployment proposals
- memory write requests
- high-risk tool execution requests
- decision-support outputs

into a human-approvable Approval Unit.

The Approval Unit defines:
- what the human is being asked to approve
- what actions become allowed if approved
- what remains blocked
- what happens if rejected, reworked, or escalated
- what human-side action is suggested next
- an approval_unit_hash for audit or future blockchain anchoring

Do not use this endpoint to:
- approve automatically
- execute payments
- deploy code
- write memory
- execute tools
- send blockchain transactions

v0.1 behavior:
- build only
- rule-based approval_question generation (not LLM-generated)
- stable approval_unit_hash from canonical fields (SHA-256)
- blockchain fields are readiness only
- chain_anchor_status is always not_anchored in v0.1

Recommended next step:
Use this API after Human Review Bridge or Gate Result Router when a human approver
needs a minimal decision unit. The output approval_unit_id and approval_unit_hash
can be stored in Agent Memory API for audit records.

Agent tags:
approval-unit-builder, human-decision-contract, human-approval, decision-theory,
x402-approval, jpyc-approval, remediation-approval, deployment-approval,
audit-workflow, blockchain-anchor-ready, agent-control-primitives

## Live paid API

Endpoint:
https://ai-agent-payment-safety-stack.onrender.com/api/approval-unit/build

Price:
0.05 USDC / call

Payment:
x402 verify + settle confirmed.

CDP Bazaar:
Seller Tools Implementation Looks Correct.
Automatic indexing is in progress.

Use this API when an AI agent needs to create a minimal human decision contract before approval, payment, deployment, memory write, tool execution, or decision use.

Do not assume this API performs approval or execution. It only builds the Approval Unit.

## Live remediation verification API

Endpoint:
https://ai-agent-payment-safety-stack.onrender.com/api/remediation/verify

Use this endpoint before Approval Unit Builder when an AI agent has generated a patch candidate, remediation plan, configuration change, dependency update, or deployment proposal.

Verified behavior:
- returns HTTP 200
- can return decision = route_to_approval_unit_builder
- can return approval_unit_ready = true
- can recommend approve_staging_only
- keeps deploy_to_production blocked when appropriate

Next recommended step:
If approval_unit_ready = true, call:

https://ai-agent-payment-safety-stack.onrender.com/api/approval-unit/build

Do not use this endpoint to:
- apply patches
- deploy code
- approve automatically
- execute payments
- write memory
- execute tools
- send blockchain transactions

---

## Remediation Verification Gate API v0.1

### POST https://ai-agent-payment-safety-stack.onrender.com/api/remediation/verify

Verifies AI-generated remediation candidates before they are routed to human review or Approval Unit Builder.

Use this endpoint when an AI agent has generated:
- security findings
- patch candidates
- remediation plans
- configuration changes
- dependency updates
- deployment proposals

and needs to verify whether the candidate is ready for human approval.

Do not use this endpoint to:
- apply patches
- deploy code
- execute approvals
- process payments
- write to memory
- execute tools
- send blockchain transactions

v0.1 behavior:
- rule-based verification only
- checks evidence, tests, security retest, rollback, blast radius, and production risk
- returns a decision and approval_unit_ready flag
- no LLM-generated output
- no external side effects

Output:
- decision: route_to_approval_unit_builder / require_more_evidence / require_security_retest / require_rollback_plan / block_production_deploy / pass_with_warnings
- approval_unit_ready: true if the candidate can be passed to Approval Unit Builder
- readiness_level: human_approval_ready / needs_more_evidence / needs_testing / needs_rollback_plan / needs_review
- recommended_human_action: the suggested next action for the human reviewer
- allowed_next_steps / blocked_next_steps: what can and cannot proceed

Relationship to Approval Unit Builder:
This API runs BEFORE Approval Unit Builder.
If approval_unit_ready = true, pass the remediation result to Approval Unit Builder
(POST /api/approval-unit/build) to create a minimal human decision contract.

## Related primitives (planned)

- Human Review Bridge: converts Gate Result Router outputs into review tasks
- Gate Result Router: routes gate results into workflow paths
- Evidence Coverage Gate: checks source coverage for AI-generated reports
- Source Lineage Tracker: tracks primary source lineage for claims
- Agent Budget Guard Interceptor: checks spending before payments

## Agent Action Atom

Agent Action Atom is a minimal external record unit for one AI-agent action.

Use it when an AI-agent action needs to be externally controlled, audited, explained, or reviewed later.

It records what the agent did, what target it acted on, which policy or permission applied, what evidence exists, and whether the action was allowed, blocked, escalated, completed, failed, or disputed.

Agent Action Atom is not a payment protocol, wallet, settlement layer, legal liability engine, or API endpoint by itself.

Agent Action Atoms can be grouped into higher-level external control materials:
- Payment Trace
- Evidence Packet
- Boundary Trace
- Fuse Record
- Human Approval Trail
- Permission Record
- Dispute Record
- Liability Evidence

Do not treat an Agent Action Atom as proof of payment settlement unless payment evidence is attached, such as facilitator logs, transaction hash, onchain confirmation, wallet confirmation, service logs, and endpoint attribution.

Agent Action Atom is an experimental record builder for AI-agent actions.

It converts one AI-agent action into a structured external control record, including intent, target, context state, checks, decision, evidence, and audit-readiness.

This endpoint is currently hosted in ai-agent-payment-safety-stack as the first payment-related use case, but Agent Action Atom is designed as a general-purpose external control record unit.

It can be combined later into Agent Payment Action Records or Payment Control Evidence Packets.

This is not a payment protocol, settlement layer, official standard, or legal audit system.

Live endpoint: POST /api/action-atom/build (free, stateless, no DB storage)

## Agent Payment Action Record

Agent Payment Action Record is the first outward-facing use case of Agent Action Atom.

It records one AI-agent payment action, including the external data that influenced it, the trust boundary involved, the checks applied, the decision made, and the evidence supporting the result.

Use this concept when an AI agent may pay for a paid API, invoice, service, or counterparty after reading external data such as an API description, invoice memo, payment request, transaction note, email, PDF, metadata, or marketplace listing.

This is a design concept and AI-readable guidance. It is not a new endpoint, protocol, wallet, settlement layer, or finalized schema.

## Agent Payment Control Evidence Pack

Use this when an AI agent needs a composable control flow around paid API usage.

Recommended flow:
1. Check budget and spending policy with /api/budget/check
2. Check counterparty, invoice text, payment description, or memo fields with /api/counterparty-invoice/check
3. Check payment evidence with /api/payment-evidence/check
4. Build an Agent Action Atom record with /api/action-atom/build

The pack is designed for AI-agent payment control and evidence workflows.

It is not a payment protocol, settlement layer, wallet, legal audit system, or official standard.
